text.title: information-architecture Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for information-architecture as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Navigation structure mirrors org chart (Marketing, Product, Engineering, Support as L1 categories)
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Users do not know or care about internal org structure. They navigate by their goals. Org-based IA forces users to guess which department owns the content they need.

failure-mode.description: Category named "Resources" or "Miscellaneous" without specific scope
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: "Resources" signals nothing about what it contains. Users cannot predict its contents and skip it in navigation. Everything needs a specific, content-accurate label.

failure-mode.description: IA change that affects URL structure shipped without SEO review
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: URL structure changes break search engine indexing and user-bookmarked links. Any IA change touching URLs requires human sign-off and redirect planning before deploy.

failure-mode.description: Agents generating sitemaps from feature lists will create IA that mirrors the feature roadmap, not the user's mental model
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Explicitly instruct to use user goal framing in prompts

failure-mode.description: Navigation label generation tends toward corporate-speak ("Solutions", "Resources", "Platform")
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require plain-language alternatives in the prompt

failure-mode.description: Agents do not spontaneously account for SEO implications of URL/category structure
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If SEO matters, add a constraint to the prompt ("categories should be primary keywords")

failure-mode.description: Human sign-off required before any IA change that affects URL structure
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Broken URLs cause SEO damage and user-bookmarked links to fail

failure-mode.description: Tree test validation is non-optional; treating agent-generated IA as final without testing
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The most common IA failure mode
